zh.lifeSummary: 當Otto I. von Weimar於大約1005年出生在德國賀林根魏瑪時，他的父親Wilhelm III. von Weimar32歲，母親Oda von der Lausitz-Ostmark-8歲。他和Adela van Leuven至少有2個女兒。他去世於1067年2月20日在德國賀林根薩阿利-爾藍-可萊斯奧拉姆爾德享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Weimar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernd, Hans, Kurt, Reinhard.
habitational name from any of several places called Weimar in Hesse and Thuringia.

variants.name: Weimer
meanings: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wini ‘friend’ + mār ‘famous’ or from another, composed of the elements wīg ‘battle, fight’ + mār ‘famous’.

variants.name: Waymer
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German   Weimar   or   Weimer  .

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

en.lifeSummary: When Otto I. von Weimar was born about 1005, in Weimar, Thuringia, Germany, his father, Wilhelm III. von Weimar, was 32 and his mother, Oda von der Lausitz-Ostmark, was -8. He had at least 2 daughters with Adela van Leuven. He died on 20 February 1067, in Orlamünde, Saale-Holzland-Kreis, Thuringia, Germany, at the age of 63.

Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Adela van Leuven. Il est décédé le 20 février 1067, à Orlamünde, Saale-Holzland-Kreis, Thüringen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Otto I. von Weimar um 1005 in Weimar, Thüringen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Wilhelm III. von Weimar 32 und seine Mutter Oda von der Lausitz-Ostmark -8. Er hatte mit Adela van Leuven mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 20. Februar 1067 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Orlamünde, Saale-Holzland-Kreis, Thüringen, Deutschland.

Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Adela van Leuven. Morì il 20 febbraio 1067, nel Orlamünde, Saale-Holzland-Kreis, Thüringen, Germania, all'età di 63 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Otto I. von Weimar(이)가 대략 1005년 독일 스u링 위마에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Wilhelm III. von Weimar(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Oda von der Lausitz-Ostmark(은)는 -8세였다. 그는 Adela van Leuven(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1067년 2월 20일 독일 스u링 사알-홀즈랜드-크레이스 올를램u드에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otto I. von Weimar родился около 1005 г. в местности вимар, тuринген, Германия, его отцу по имени Wilhelm III. von Weimar было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Oda von der Lausitz-Ostmark, -8. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Adela van Leuven. Он умер 20 февраля 1067 г. в местности орлэмuнд, саеиле-холзланд-крис, тuринген, Германия в возрасте 63 лет.
